Vocational School

About MYO

MENU

The aim of our Vocational School is to train qualified personnel needed by the sectors in the fields we provide education and entrepreneurs who will establish their own businesses.

Course programs and contents are shaped in line with the needs of the sectors. In addition to valuable academicians, the academic staff includes distinguished practitioners who are experts in their fields and leading managers of the sector.

In our university, in addition to theoretical courses; great importance is also given to practice. In the Sector Cooperation programs prepared in accordance with the 3+1 format, our students complete their education in business life by reinforcing the education they receive at the university with the practical training they receive at the workplace. In addition, a large part of the courses are carried out practically in our modern laboratories and workshops.

Our students are prepared for business life with the "Preparation for Business Life" course, and students are provided with internships in reputable companies. Our students are supported to both study and work part-time, and this issue is taken into consideration in their course planning.

Double Major and Minor Application: Students of our Vocational School have the right to do a double major. Our students who successfully participate in the double major program have the right to two separate diplomas for both majors. Our students also have the right to do a minor. Our students who successfully participate in the minor program have the right to receive a certificate for a different program in addition to their major diplomas.

Career opportunities: Our Career Center supports our students not only during their university education but also in their post-university career planning through protocols and cooperation agreements with sector organizations and provides conscious and productive contributions to their future.

Vertical Transfer Opportunities: Our graduates have the opportunity to continue their higher education at the undergraduate level by taking the Vertical Transfer Exam (DGS). Students who graduate from our school show great success in the DGS exam every year.
